definition: to state an idea clearly and confidently
example: The writer asserts that everyone deserves safety.

definition: to suggest an idea without saying it directly
example: The final line implies that the speaker feels hopeful.

definition: to give special importance to something
example: The speaker emphasises the need for change.

definition: to say or show the opposite of something
example: This evidence contradicts the first claim.

definition: to compare things by showing differences
example: Contrast the two characters in your answer.

definition: to explain something with examples or pictures
example: The example illustrates the main idea clearly.

definition: to show or describe something
example: The poem depicts a quiet coastal town.

definition: to communicate an idea or feeling
example: The author conveys sadness through imagery.

definition: to create or set something up
example: The opening scene establishes the mood.

definition: to make an idea stronger
example: The second quote reinforces the argument.

definition: the way parts are arranged
example: The structure of the essay is clear.

definition: a set of events or steps in order
example: Put the events in the correct sequence.

definition: a person's way of thinking about something
example: The article presents a strong viewpoint.

definition: a statement that may need evidence
example: Support your claim with a quote.

definition: an argument against another argument
example: Include a counterargument in your persuasive essay.

definition: making sense because ideas connect well
example: A logical paragraph is easy to follow.

definition: smooth and easy to read or say
example: Her presentation was fluent and confident.

definition: suitable for serious school or work situations
example: Use formal language in an essay.

definition: relaxed and casual in style
example: Text messages often use informal language.

definition: the level of formality in language
example: Choose a register that suits the audience.

definition: a set of steps for doing something
example: Follow the procedure during the experiment.

definition: the equipment used for a science experiment

definition: to keep something the same in an experiment
example: Control the amount of water each plant receives.

definition: something that stays the same
example: Temperature was kept constant during the test.

definition: based on a fair method and useful evidence
example: A valid experiment tests only one variable.

definition: something different from the expected pattern
example: One result was an anomaly and needed checking.

definition: to carry out an activity or experiment
example: We conducted the test three times.

definition: a type of material with particular properties
example: Water is a common substance in experiments.

definition: a quality that something has
example: Hardness is a property of some minerals.

definition: the amount of mass in a certain volume
example: Oil has a lower density than water.

definition: to change from liquid to gas
example: Water will evaporate faster in sunlight.

definition: to change from gas to liquid
example: Water vapour can condense on a cold surface.

definition: the force that pulls objects toward each other
example: Gravity pulls objects toward Earth.

definition: a force that slows movement between surfaces
example: Friction helps shoes grip the ground.

definition: the place where an organism lives
example: A rainforest is a habitat for many animals.

definition: a feature that helps an organism survive
example: Thick fur is an adaptation for cold climates.

definition: the process plants use to make food from light
example: Photosynthesis happens in the leaves of plants.

definition: the process cells use to release energy
example: Respiration releases energy from glucose.

definition: a substance that living things need to grow
example: Plants need nutrients from the soil.

definition: having the same value
example: Fifty percent is equivalent to one half.

definition: a whole number that can be positive negative or zero
example: Negative three is an integer.

definition: a number made by multiplying another number
example: Twelve is a multiple of three.

definition: a number that divides exactly into another number
example: Six is a factor of twenty four.

definition: a number with exactly two factors
example: Seven is a prime number.

definition: to change a number to a nearby simpler number
example: Round the answer to two decimal places.

definition: to replace a value with another value
example: Substitute x equals four into the formula.

definition: forming a straight line or simple sequence
example: A linear graph has a constant gradient.

definition: a reference line on a graph

definition: the size system used on a graph
example: Choose a suitable scale for the graph.

definition: to cross or meet at a point
example: The two lines intersect at the origin.

definition: the space between two lines that meet
example: Measure the angle with a protractor.

definition: a shape with three sides
example: A triangle has three angles.

definition: a balanced match on both sides
example: The shape has line symmetry.

definition: a number that describes information
example: Use a statistic to support your claim.

definition: a value that represents a set of numbers
example: Find the average score for the class.

definition: the middle value in an ordered data set
example: The median is less affected by one very high value.

definition: coming directly from the time or event
example: A diary can be a primary source.

definition: based on other sources or later analysis

definition: unfairly supporting one side

definition: the order in which events happened
example: Create a chronology of the main events.

definition: a large group of lands ruled by one power
example: The Roman Empire controlled many regions.

definition: a place controlled by another country

definition: the joining of states under one government

definition: a set of basic rules for government

definition: a group of elected people who make laws

definition: a person who legally belongs to a country

definition: fair treatment under rules or law

definition: an unfair difference between groups

definition: related to a city

definition: related to the countryside

definition: an area with particular features

definition: the usual weather conditions in a place

definition: the number of people living in a place

definition: to suggest the best choice

definition: to make sure something is true

definition: to stop a planned event

definition: to arrange a new time for something

definition: to take something and return it later

definition: to give something for someone to return

definition: money returned after a purchase

definition: to put a new thing in place of another

definition: able to be used or seen

definition: easy and suitable for a situation

definition: suitable for a situation
example: Use appropriate language in an email.

definition: needing quick attention

definition: able to wait calmly
example: Be patient when learning a hard topic.

definition: feeling ashamed or uncomfortable
example: He felt embarrassed after forgetting his lines.

definition: unhappy because something was not as hoped

definition: pleased because of an achievement
example: She felt proud after finishing the project.

definition: feeling thankful

definition: separated from others

definition: the feeling of being accepted in a group

definition: a way of thinking about learning or life

definition: the ability to control behaviour and keep working

definition: the ability to focus attention

definition: the act of studying again before a test

definition: to learn something so you can remember it

definition: to decide what is most important
example: Prioritise the assignment due tomorrow.

meaningZh: 可管理的 / 能应付的
definition: small or clear enough to deal with

definition: working well without wasting time
